domain: Chiloé palafito stilt-house (Chiloé Island Patagonia; wooden-pile foundation with multi-tier dwelling — water-line pier + ground-floor living + attic storage — flexible piles accommodating tidal flux up to 10m; multi-front design with sea-dock terrace + street access; communal wooden-bridge interconnections; native woods luma/cypress/alerce)
specific_mechanism: CHILOTE-PALAFITO: three-tier persistent memory architecture with flexible-pile foundation embedding. (1) Tier-0 Pier: fastest HBM-resident attention KV-cache, evicted every K tokens. (2) Tier-1 Dwelling: mid-context DRAM cache, importance-LRU evicted. (3) Tier-2 Attic: long-term persistent parametric memory, periodic compression. (4) Flexible-pile foundation: shared low-rank base embedding F_base anchored across tiers; F_base adapts to tidal data-distribution shift via small ΔF update. (5) Wooden-bridge cross-tier links: explicit read/write paths between (Tier-0,Tier-1) and (Tier-1,Tier-2) via gated sliding-window attention.
llm_application: CHILOTE-PALAFITO hierarchical memory for long-context LLM serving: 3-tier HBM/DRAM/persistent-parametric with flexible shared base-embedding + gated cross-tier bridges + tidal-flux-adaptive eviction. Differs from Tutti 2605.03375 (3-tier HBM-DRAM-SSD storage but no shared-base flexible-pile embedding), Strata 2508.18572 (GPU-assisted I/O hierarchical caching but no flexible base or tidal adaptation), MTDS 2025 (multi-tier dynamic storage with adaptive eviction but no shared base embedding), LMCache (batched pipelined cache load but no tier-bandwidth tiering with base anchor). Differs from R408 ICELANDIC-CHAIN (daisy-chain serial memory, not tier persistence), R428 WAVE-BOWL-CACHE (concentric-ring KV priority, not tier persistence), R442 ADINKRA-SYMBOL-LM (canonical 128-symbol catalog, not tier persistence) by EXPLICIT FLEXIBLE-PILE BASE-EMBEDDING ANCHOR + WOODEN-BRIDGE CROSS-TIER GATED ATTENTION.
novelty_claim: Tutti + Strata + MTDS + LMCache cover multi-tier KV-cache LLM memory hierarchy paradigm. CHILOTE-PALAFITO adds (a) shared flexible-pile base-embedding F_base anchoring across tiers + (b) wooden-bridge gated cross-tier read/write paths + (c) tidal-flux distribution-shift adaptation. Recombination — base-embeddings and gated attention are mainstream.
